treatment_information.dosage_or_details: Started at 0.5 g/kg every 6 hours, increased to 2 g/kg every 6 hours over the next week
treatment_information.treatment_response: Improvement in pulmonary hypertension, decrease in oxygen requirement from 80% to 40%, decrease in PIP from 30 to 22, decrease in OI from 23.7 to 4.4
Slight decrease in oxygen requirement to 80% and decrease of the OI to 23.7
Required reintubation the next day for increasing oxygen requirement and CO2 retention

treatment_information.dosage_or_details: Started at 10 cm H2O, decreased over the next 3 months to 3 cm H2O

treatment_timeline.event: Intubated for respiratory distress and transferred to the neonatal intensive care unit
Extubated to nasopharyngeal continuous positive airway pressure (CPAP)
Reintubated for increasing oxygen requirement and CO2 retention
Inhaled nitric oxide (iNO) commenced at 20 ppm
Sildenafil commenced at a dose of 0.5 g/kg every 6 hours
Sildenafil dose increased to 2 g/kg every 6 hours
Successfully extubated to a CPAP of 10 cm H2O
CPAP discontinued, remained on oxygen 0.5 L/min via subnasal prongs

patient_outcome.final_status: No echocardiographic evidence of pulmonary hypertension, requires glasses for myopia, hearing aids for severe hearing impairment, oxygen for intercurrent respiratory tract infections
patient_outcome.follow_up_details: Planned to have a repair of the cleft palate in the next year
patient_outcome.complications: Upper airway obstruction related to the cleft palate, laryngomalacia
patient_outcome.long_term_prognosis: Motor development is delayed, communicates with sign language, no current concerns regarding mental development
patient_outcome.quality_of_life: Requires glasses for myopia, hearing aids for severe hearing impairment, oxygen for intercurrent respiratory tract infections
